meta.title: BMW 330e Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 330e engine — B48 petrol and hybrid system. F30 and G20 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 330e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned B48 engine for the 330e typically costs £3,500–£6,500 supply only depending on generation, with the G20 version sitting at the higher end. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. The hybrid system components (battery, electric motor) are additional costs if they need replacement.

The B48 petrol engine is rated ★★★★☆ — it's one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. However, the 330e's hybrid system adds complexity: battery degradation is expected over time, and electric motor cooling issues can occur. The G20 generation (2019+) offers improved battery capacity and range over the F30.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 330e?

acceptedAnswer.text: Battery degradation (range loss), electric motor and charging system issues, and B48 coolant system failures are the most common. The B48 itself is reliable, but the hybrid system demands specialist attention.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 330e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a £8,000–£20,000 330e, a £4,500 recon engine is a manageable 22–56% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a well-maintained example. However, if the hybrid battery also needs replacement (£6,000–£12,000), assess carefully. For a G20 with high retained values, replacement is almost always viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 330e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 330e uses the B48 2.0-litre petrol engine combined with an electric motor. The badge signifies the plug-in hybrid model. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the 330e's hybrid battery, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The hybrid battery is separate from the engine. Cell-level replacement or module replacement is possible, costing £2,000–£5,000. If the entire battery pack needs replacement, it's significantly more expensive (£6,000–£12,000). BMW's 8-year/100,000-mile battery warranty provides some protection.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 330e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with documented oil-change intervals
* Battery health report available — check capacity against new (aim for >85% of original)
* Evidence of regular use — hybrid systems prefer regular charging and driving
* Post-2023 build for the larger 19.5kWh battery and improved range
avoidIf: * Drivetrain warning lights with no diagnosis
* Significantly reduced electric range compared to new (below 70% of original)
* No service history — the B48 is reliable but the hybrid system demands maintenance
* Charging issues — the car won't charge or takes significantly longer than expected

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 330e is a compelling hybrid, combining the proven B48 petrol engine with an efficient electric motor. The B48 itself is one of BMW's most reliable engines, but the hybrid system adds complexity. Buy on battery health and service history — these are not just another 3 Series.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 330e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Battery Health — What is the current electric range compared to new? Check the battery health report
* Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain wear — far less common on B48 than N47)
* Service History — Including which engine era (F30 B48 or G20 B48) and hybrid system maintenance
* Charging System — Does the car charge correctly? Any error messages?
* Drivetrain Warning — Any warning lights on the dash? Have codes been read?
* Electric Range — Test the electric range in real conditions; significant loss indicates battery degradation
* Regenerative Braking — Does it function correctly? Any unusual noises from the transmission?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures; the B48 should pass easily if well-maintained
